vulnerabilities.description: strcpy copies user-controlled input into a fixed 100-byte stack buffer without bounds checking, enabling stack-based buffer overflow.
printf uses buffer (populated from untrusted input) directly as the format string, allowing format string injection to read or corrupt memory.
count * sizeof(int) overflows when count exceeds INT_MAX/sizeof(int), producing a smaller-than-expected size passed to malloc.
arr[i] writes count elements into a heap buffer allocated with the wrapped (too-small) size, causing heap-based buffer overflow.
argv[1] is passed to vulnerable_function without validating its length, allowing arbitrarily long input to reach the fixed-size buffer copy.
